name: Rosettes
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: TASTE OF HOME, DEC/JAN 1996
In a mixing bowl, beat eggs and sugar; stir in milk and vanilla. Combine flour and salt; add to batter and beat until smooth. Heat cooking oil to 365 degrees Fahrenheit in a deep fat fryer or electric skillet. Place rosette iron into hot oil, then dip into batter three-fourths up on sides of the iron (do not let batter run over the top). Immediately place rosette into hot oil; loosen with fork and remove iron. Fry one to two minutes per side or until golden brown. Remove to a wire rack covered with paper towel. Repeat with remaining batter for 4-5 dozen. For icing, combine sugar, vanilla, and enough water to make a dipping consistency. Dip edges of rosettes into icing; dry on wire racks.
